etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 各(各) (gè) -> river
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") indicates that the character relates to water, fitting for a river. The phonetic part 各(各) (gè) provides the sound, not contributing directly to the meaning.

examples.chinese: 洛河流经这座城市(洛河流經這座城市)。
examples.english: The Luo River flows through this city.

examples.chinese: 在古代，洛水被视为神圣之地(在古代，洛水被視為神聖之地)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gǔdài, luòshuǐ bèi shì wéi shénshèng zhī dì.
examples.english: In ancient times, the Luo River was considered a sacred place.

examples.chinese: 我曾沿着洛河泛舟(我曾沿着洛河泛舟)，欣赏两岸的美景(欣賞兩岸的美景)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ céng yánzhe Luòhé fànzhōu, xīnshǎng liǎng'àn de měijǐng.
examples.english: I once boated along the Luo River, enjoying the scenery on both sides.
